description: §16.2 Workflow-tier dashboard 3/5 — Kestra SSO-integrated user-facing flow execution. Reads from workflow.workflow_runs entries where source='kestra' plus the support_packet/escalation_routing dispatcher envelopes.

panels.title: Kestra flow executions/min by namespace
panels.description: Throughput across all configured Kestra flows. The georag.support namespace dominates today (support_packet, escalation_routing). Per-namespace breakdown surfaces noisy-neighbour patterns.

panels.title: Flow execution state distribution
panels.description: Live distribution of CREATED / RUNNING / SUCCESS / FAILED / KILLED states. FAILED + KILLED rate climbing means an upstream system is unreliable.

panels.title: FastAPI → Kestra dispatch latency p95 (s)
panels.description: Per-request POST /api/v1/executions/{ns}/{flow} latency from the support_packet dispatcher. Phase G.5 set KESTRA_HTTP_TIMEOUT_S=5; sustained p95 > 2s suggests Kestra worker saturation.

panels.title: Dispatch failure reasons (req/s)
panels.description: Phase G.5 dispatcher result envelope. reason ∈ {kestra_disabled, kestra_http_error, kestra_network_error}. kestra_disabled = config-driven no-op; kestra_http_error / kestra_network_error = real failures.

panels.title: Kestra worker availability (%)
panels.description: Rolling 1h availability — fraction of dispatch attempts that succeeded. < 99% sustained = the Hikari pool / postgres connectivity issue Kyle hit yesterday is recurring.

panels.title: Pending executions (queue depth)
panels.description: Kestra-side queue depth — executions in CREATED or RUNNING state. Sustained growth = worker can't keep up with dispatch rate; consider scaling kestra-worker.

title: GeoRAG — Workflows (Kestra)
